mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate cuts are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.0 percent, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions and rising recession probability, which suppresses corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The signal produces positive 20-day forward returns in more than 55 percent of triggered instances, or the out-of-sample hit rate falls below 40 percent over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts confirm broad-based consumer spending strength, which directly boosts corporate revenue forecasts and generates positive equity returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce a statistically significant positive alpha (t-stat < 1.96) over 60-day horizons, or the interaction term loses predictive power in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month growth in the index of industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and output, which supports near-term corporate profit margins and yields positive short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The mechanism generates negative 5-day forward returns in over 60 percent of observations during mid-cycle expansions, or the rank correlation drops below zero in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly real GDP growth directly increases aggregate corporate earnings and economic activity, which systematically lifts equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: The signal yields a negative information coefficient over any rolling 4-quarter window, or 60-day forward returns are uncorrelated with GDP growth revisions.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index annualized growth exceeds 2.5 percent, it triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity discount factors and forces negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive in more than 50 percent of high-inflation trigger months, or the predictive accuracy of the threshold signal is indistinguishable from random chance.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate indicates a robust labor market expansion, which sustains consumer spending power and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over one month.
failure_condition: The interaction model produces a negative Sharpe ratio over 20-day horizons, or the joint labor signal fails to outperform a baseline market-neutral strategy in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month decline in housing starts signals a leading contraction in construction activity and consumer leverage, which drags down cyclical equity sectors and produces negative short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The signal generates positive 5-day forward returns in over 55 percent of late-cycle observations, or the housing decline fails to predict equity drawdowns with statistical significance.
